sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
The **Notion connector** integrates with Notion workspaces via the Notion API v1. It enables Shielva to sync pages, databases, and rich block content in real-time.

This connector uses Internal Integration Token authentication (`secret_...`), granting access only to pages explicitly shared with the integration.

**Key capabilities:**
- Search all accessible pages and databases via `POST /search` with cursor-based pagination
- Fetch individual pages with full metadata
- Recursively fetch block content (paragraphs, headings, lists, code, callouts, etc.)
- Retrieve database schemas and query database rows
- Stable document IDs via SHA-256(page_id) for deduplication across syncs
- Incremental sync via cursor pagination

children.title: Connector Type
children.content: `notion` — registered in ACP as **Notion**.

children.title: Provider
children.content: Notion Labs Inc. (Notion API v1 — `https://api.notion.com/v1/`)

children.title: Supported Operations
children.content:
| Operation | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `install()` | Validates integration_token presence; returns HEALTHY/CONNECTED or OFFLINE/MISSING_CREDENTIALS |
| `health_check()` | Calls `GET /users/me` to verify token; returns bot name in message |
| `sync()` | Searches all pages + databases, fetches block content, normalizes to ConnectorDocuments |
| `search(query, filter_type)` | Paginated `POST /search` returning pages and/or databases |
| `get_page(page_id)` | `GET /pages/{page_id}` for a single page |
| `get_page_content(page_id)` | `GET /blocks/{page_id}/children` recursive — full block tree |
| `get_database(database_id)` | `GET /databases/{database_id}` for a database schema |
| `query_database(database_id, filter)` | Paginated `POST /databases/{database_id}/query` |

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content: The connector uses **Internal Integration Token** authentication — a single `secret_...` token granted when you create a Notion integration.

children.title: Auth Type
children.content: `api_key` — create an integration at [notion.so/my-integrations](https://www.notion.so/my-integrations), then copy the Internal Integration Token.

children.title: Install Fields
children.content:
| Field | Key | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|-----|------|----------|-------------|
| Integration Token | `integration_token` | password | Yes | Notion Internal Integration Token (secret_...) |

children.title: Required Integration Capabilities
children.content:
| Capability | Purpose |
|------------|---------|
| Read content | Access pages and databases shared with the integration |
| Read user information | Optional — retrieve user metadata |

children.title: Page Sharing
children.content:
Notion integrations only access pages explicitly shared with them. For each page or database to sync:
1. Open the page in Notion
2. Click **···** → **Add connections**
3. Select your integration

To sync an entire workspace, share the workspace root page.

children.title: Token Security
children.content: Integration tokens do not expire unless regenerated or the integration is deleted. Shielva stores the token AES-256-GCM encrypted at rest via the vault. Tokens can be regenerated at any time from the Notion integration settings.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content: All configuration is stored in the connector's `config` dict (encrypted at rest by Shielva vault).

children.title: Config Keys
children.content:
| Key | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| `integration_token` | Notion Internal Integration Token (secret_...) |

children.title: API Version
children.content: All requests include the `Notion-Version: 2022-06-28` header as required by the Notion API.

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All methods are implemented in `connector.py` and delegate HTTP calls to `NotionHTTPClient` in `client/http_client.py`.

children.title: install()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def install(self) -> InstallResult`

Checks that `integration_token` is present in config. Does not make network calls — token validity is confirmed at `health_check()`.

**Returns:**
- `InstallResult(health=HEALTHY, auth_status=CONNECTED)` when integration_token is present
- `InstallResult(health=OFFLINE, auth_status=MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` when integration_token is absent or empty

children.title: health_check()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def health_check(self) -> HealthCheckResult`

Calls `GET https://api.notion.com/v1/users/me` to verify the token. Returns the bot name in the message.

**Returns:**
- `HealthCheckResult(health=HEALTHY, auth_status=CONNECTED, message='Connected — bot: My Bot')`
- `HealthCheckResult(health=DEGRADED, auth_status=INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` on 401/403 errors
- `HealthCheckResult(health=DEGRADED, auth_status=FAILED)` on network errors

children.title: sync()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def sync(self, full=False, since=None, kb_id='') -> SyncResult`

Searches all accessible pages and databases via `POST /search`, then for each page fetches block children for content enrichment. Normalizes all objects to ConnectorDocuments.

**Parameters:**
- `full` — reserved for future incremental sync support
- `since` — reserved for future timestamp-filtered sync
- `kb_id` — knowledge base ID for ingestion (SDK mode only)

**Returns:** `SyncResult` with counts of found/synced/failed documents.

Status is `COMPLETED` when no failures occur, `PARTIAL` when some objects fail normalization, `FAILED` when the search API call fails entirely.

children.title: search()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def search(self, query='', filter_type=None) -> list[dict]`

Paginated `POST /search`. Follows `has_more` + `next_cursor` until exhausted.

**Parameters:**
- `query` — text search string (empty = all accessible objects)
- `filter_type` — `'page'` or `'database'` to restrict result types, or `None` for both

**Returns:** List of Notion page and database objects.

children.title: get_page()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def get_page(self, page_id: str) -> dict`

Fetches a single page via `GET /pages/{page_id}`.

**Returns:** Notion page object with properties.

**Raises:** `NotionNotFoundError` if the page does not exist or is not shared with the integration.

children.title: get_page_content()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def get_page_content(self, page_id: str) -> list[dict]`

Recursively fetches all block children via `GET /blocks/{page_id}/children`. Follows `has_more` + `next_cursor` at each nesting level.

**Returns:** Flat list of all block objects from the page tree, including nested children.

Blocks with `has_children=True` are automatically traversed.

children.title: get_database()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def get_database(self, database_id: str) -> dict`

Fetches a database schema via `GET /databases/{database_id}`.

**Returns:** Notion database object with property schema.

**Raises:** `NotionNotFoundError` if the database does not exist or is not shared.

children.title: query_database()
children.content:
**Signature:** `async def query_database(self, database_id: str, filter=None) -> list[dict]`

Paginated `POST /databases/{database_id}/query`. Returns all rows (pages) matching the filter.

**Parameters:**
- `database_id` — Notion database ID
- `filter` — optional Notion filter object (see [Notion Filter docs](https://developers.notion.com/reference/post-database-query-filter))

**Returns:** List of page objects (database rows) matching the filter.

sections.title: HTTP Client
sections.content: All HTTP calls are encapsulated in `NotionHTTPClient` (`client/http_client.py`) using `aiohttp`. The connector never calls HTTP directly.

children.title: Methods
children.content:
| Method | Notion API Call | Description |
|--------|----------------|-------------|
| `get_user_me(token)` | `GET /users/me` | Verify token, returns bot user object |
| `search(token, query, filter_type, start_cursor, page_size)` | `POST /search` | Search pages and databases with cursor pagination |
| `get_page(token, page_id)` | `GET /pages/{page_id}` | Single page with properties |
| `get_block_children(token, block_id, start_cursor, page_size)` | `GET /blocks/{block_id}/children` | Child blocks of a page or block |
| `get_database(token, database_id)` | `GET /databases/{database_id}` | Database schema |
| `query_database(token, database_id, filter_obj, start_cursor, page_size)` | `POST /databases/{database_id}/query` | Query database rows |

children.title: Request Headers
children.content:
Every request includes:
```
Authorization: Bearer {integration_token}
Notion-Version: 2022-06-28
Content-Type: application/json
```

children.title: Pagination
children.content:
The Notion API uses cursor-based pagination. All list endpoints return:
```json
{
  "results": [...],
  "has_more": true,
  "next_cursor": "cursor-string"
}
```
The connector follows `has_more` + `next_cursor` until `has_more` is false or `next_cursor` is null.

children.title: Error Mapping
children.content:
HTTP status codes are mapped to typed exceptions:

| HTTP Status | Exception |
|-------------|----------|
| 401 | `NotionAuthError` |
| 403 | `NotionAuthError` |
| 404 | `NotionNotFoundError` |
| 429 | `NotionRateLimitError` |
| Connection/timeout | `NotionNetworkError` |
| 5xx / other | `NotionError` |

children.title: Retry Logic
children.content:
`with_retry()` in `helpers/utils.py` wraps API calls with exponential back-off:
- `max_attempts=3` by default
- Back-off: 1s, 2s (doubles each attempt)
- Skips retry on `NotionAuthError` — re-installing with a valid token is required
- Skips retry on `NotionNotFoundError` — the resource does not exist
- Retries on `NotionError`, `NotionNetworkError`, `NotionRateLimitError`

sections.title: Data Model
sections.content: Normalized documents produced by `normalize_page()` and `normalize_database()` in `helpers/utils.py`.

children.title: Page ConnectorDocument
children.content:
```json
{
  "id": "<sha256(page_id)[:16]>",
  "type": "notion_page",
  "title": "My Test Page",
  "content": "Title: My Test Page\nURL: https://www.notion.so/my-test-page\nCreated: 2024-01-01T00:00:00.000Z\nLast edited: 2024-06-01T00:00:00.000Z\nParent type: workspace\n--- Content ---\nSome paragraph text",
  "metadata": {
    "page_id": "page-1234-abcd-5678-efgh",
    "url": "https://www.notion.so/my-test-page",
    "created_time": "2024-01-01T00:00:00.000Z",
    "last_edited_time": "2024-06-01T00:00:00.000Z",
    "archived": false,
    "parent_type": "workspace",
    "object_type": "page",
    "connector_id": "notion_connector",
    "tenant_id": "Tenant-f9184cb7",
    "source": "notion"
  }
}
```

children.title: Database ConnectorDocument
children.content:
```json
{
  "id": "<sha256(database_id)[:16]>",
  "type": "notion_database",
  "title": "My Test Database",
  "content": "Database: My Test Database\nURL: https://www.notion.so/my-test-db\nCreated: 2024-01-01T00:00:00.000Z\nProperties: Name, Status, Due",
  "metadata": {
    "database_id": "db-1234-abcd-5678-efgh",
    "url": "https://www.notion.so/my-test-db",
    "created_time": "2024-01-01T00:00:00.000Z",
    "last_edited_time": "2024-06-01T00:00:00.000Z",
    "archived": false,
    "property_names": ["Name", "Status", "Due"],
    "object_type": "database",
    "connector_id": "notion_connector",
    "tenant_id": "Tenant-f9184cb7",
    "source": "notion"
  }
}
```

children.title: Document ID Stability
children.content: The `id` field is `SHA-256(notion_object_id)` truncated to 16 hex characters. This is stable across syncs — the same Notion page or database always produces the same Shielva document ID, enabling upsert deduplication without storing seen-id lists.

children.title: Block Content Rendering
children.content:
Block content is flattened to plain text in `_block_to_text()`:

| Block Type | Rendered As |
|------------|-------------|
| `paragraph` | Plain text |
| `heading_1` | `# Heading` |
| `heading_2` | `## Heading` |
| `heading_3` | `### Heading` |
| `bulleted_list_item` | `- item` |
| `numbered_list_item` | `1. item` |
| `to_do` | `[ ] task` or `[x] done` |
| `quote` | `> quote` |
| `callout` | `> callout` |
| `code` | ` ```lang\ncode\n``` ` |
| `divider` | `---` |
| `equation` | LaTeX expression |

sections.title: Exception Hierarchy
sections.content:
All exceptions inherit from `NotionError` (base) in `exceptions.py`:

```
NotionError (base)
├── NotionAuthError         — 401, 403 responses
├── NotionNetworkError      — connectivity failures, aiohttp.ClientError
├── NotionRateLimitError    — 429 Too Many Requests
└── NotionNotFoundError     — 404 Not Found
```

Callers can catch the base `NotionError` or specific subclasses for finer control.

children.title: Common Errors
children.content:
| Error | Cause | Fix |
|-------|-------|-----|
| `401 Unauthorized` | Invalid or expired token | Re-copy the Integration Token from notion.so/my-integrations |
| `403 Forbidden` | Integration not shared with the page | Open the page → **···** → **Add connections** → select your integration |
| `404 Not Found` | Page or database does not exist | Verify the ID and that the page is shared with the integration |
| `429 Too Many Requests` | Rate limit exceeded | Connector retries automatically with exponential back-off |
| No pages returned | No pages shared with integration | Share at least one page or workspace root with the integration |

children.title: Testing
children.content:
Run the full test suite (112 tests, all mocked — no live credentials needed):

```bash
cd /Users/vivekvarshavaishvik/Documents/client_dir/notion_connector
source "/Volumes/V3-SSD/Shielva Project Dirs/.venv/bin/activate"
python -m pytest tests/ -v
```

All 112 tests must pass before registering the connector.
